name: Euler diagrams
longDescription: An Euler diagram is a diagrammatic means of representing sets and their relationships. Unlike Venn diagrams, which show all possible relations between different sets, the Euler diagram shows only the existing relationships.

name: Venn diagrams
longDescription: In Venn diagrams the curves are overlapped in every possible way, showing all possible relations between the sets. Unlike the Euler diagram, Venn diagrams can show impossible, non existant, or theoretical relationships.

name: Venn diagrams that are also Euler diagrams
longDescription: In some cases, when all existing relationships describe all possible relationships, the Euler diagram will be identical to the Venn diagram.
